summary: Five critical regulatory and operational milestones within May 2026–May 2027: Abu Dhabi commercial launch (Q3 2026, 75% prob), White House eIPP pre-certification revenue operations (H2 2026, 80% prob), FAA Type Certification (Q4 2026–Q1 2027, 65% prob), USAF aircraft deliveries (H2 2026–H1 2027, 60% prob), and production ramp confirmation (2026–2027, 50% prob). All events aligned to unlock Commercial Revenue Launch and Type Certification drivers identified in A2.3.

rows.label: Abu Dhabi Commercial Operations Launch
rows.value: First paying passenger flights in Abu Dhabi under UAE Restricted Type Certificate; validates first revenue-generating operation

extras.event: Archer Midnight aircraft begins commercial eVTOL passenger service in Abu Dhabi with Abu Dhabi Aviation (ADA) as launch customer
extras.driver_impact: Commercial Revenue Launch (direct unlock: first revenue monetization)
extras.regulatory_milestone: UAE GCAA Restricted Type Certificate approval; faster pathway than US Type Cert

rows.label: White House eVTOL Integration Pilot Program Revenue Operations
rows.value: Pre-certification commercial revenue flights launched in Texas, Florida, and New York under DOT/FAA eIPP; enables revenue before Type Cert completion

extras.event: Archer begins revenue-generating eVTOL flights in three US states under White House eIPP framework, which permits commercial operations before final FAA Type Certification
extras.timing: H2 2026 (July–December 2026), with operations groundwork beginning immediately
extras.driver_impact: Commercial Revenue Launch (direct unlock: US pre-cert revenue pathway)
extras.regulatory_basis: White House executive order 'Unleashing American Drone Dominance' (June 2025); DOT/FAA selection March 9, 2026
extras.geographic_scope: Texas (TXDOT partnership), Florida (FDOT partnership), New York (Port Authority partnership)

rows.label: FAA Type Certification Issuance
rows.value: Final FAA Type Certificate issued for Midnight aircraft; enables unlimited US commercial passenger operations under Part 23 amended powered-lift standards

extras.event: Archer receives FAA Type Certification, completing commercial regulatory unlock for US operations
extras.timing: Q4 2026 – Q1 2027 (October 2026 – March 2027), estimated based on Joby Stage 4 completion (March 2026) and 6–12 month competitive lag
extras.driver_impact: FAA Type Certification Completion (direct fulfillment; necessary for commercial scaling)
extras.current_status: Archer achieved 100% Means of Compliance acceptance (May 2026); currently in Type Inspection Authorization (TIA) phase
extras.regulatory_support: FAA Part 194 (operating rule) finalized March 2026; Congressional bill (Feb 16, 2026) accelerates 270-day rule review timelines
extras.competitive_benchmark: Joby Stage 4 completed late March 2026; Archer estimated 6–12 months behind

rows.label: USAF Contract Execution & Aircraft Deliveries
rows.value: $142M USAF contract: delivery of up to six Midnight aircraft with pilot training, certification test reports, and maintenance operations development

extras.event: Archer delivers first Midnight aircraft to USAF under existing contract; validates defense partnership and generates initial revenue
extras.timing: H2 2026 – Q1 2027 (July 2026 – March 2027), aligned with USAF pilot program milestones on track for 2026
extras.driver_impact: Commercial Revenue Launch (proof-of-concept revenue from USAF partnership)
extras.contract_scope: Up to 6 Midnight aircraft; flight test data, certification test reports, pilot training, maintenance/repair operations
extras.execution_risk: Aircraft delivery dependent on production ramp and USAF operational readiness; moderate execution risk

rows.label: Production Ramp & Revenue Scale Confirmation
rows.value: Archer achieves 500 aircraft production target in 2026 and begins 650 aircraft/year cadence in 2027 at 40–50% gross margins; validates manufacturing readiness and capital efficiency

extras.event: Archer demonstrates ability to deliver aircraft at scale necessary to support Abu Dhabi, eIPP, and United Airlines operations in parallel
extras.timing: Q4 2026 & Q1–Q2 2027 (December 2026 – June 2027), evaluated through quarterly earnings releases
extras.driver_impact: All three A2.3 drivers (Revenue Launch, Type Cert, Battery & Capital): production ramp gates revenue scaling and validates capital efficiency
extras.production_targets: 2026: 500 aircraft annually; 2027: 650 aircraft annually at 40–50% gross margin = $500–630M gross profit
extras.manufacturing_support: Stellantis $400M manufacturing capital through 2030; Georgia and California facilities operational as of Q4 2025
extras.execution_risk: High risk (50% probability) due to aggressive ramp from 6 aircraft (Aug 2025) baseline; battery supply chain and labor constraints primary concerns
extras.financial_runway: Archer $2B+ liquidity as of May 2026; 18–24 month cash runway with eIPP + Abu Dhabi revenue offsets
